webLinks.linkText: Jervis Bay Road and Princes Highway intersection upgrade

properties.otherAdvice:
JERVIS BAY ROAD INTERSECTION UPGRADE

Construction work is underway, including major earthworks and embankment construction; bridge works and retaining wall construction; road surface and ramp construction; utility relocations; and drainage upgrades.

Changes to traffic conditions. including lane and shoulder closures, will be in place at various times along the Princes Hwy and other nearby roads as a result of the Jervis Bay intersection upgrade project.

Motorists are advised to allow approximately 5 minutes of additional travel time when traffic control is active.

Single-lane closure will be in place.

Traffic controllers will be on site to direct motorists.

Motorists are advised to allow approximately 5 minutes of additional travel time.

Motorists are advised to allow approximately 10 minutes of additional travel time between Wellington and Dubbo.

Traffic controllers will be on site to direct motorists. Intermittent alternating (stop/slow) traffic conditions will be in place to allow motorists to pass in one direction at a time. Motorists are advised to allow approximately 5 minutes of additional travel time.

MOUNT OUSLEY INTERCHANGE PROJECT

Motorists are advised of changed traffic conditions while work is carried out on the Mount Ousley interchange project.

Road shoulder, single lane closures and stop/slow traffic conditions will be in place as required on:

  • M1 Princes Mwy between Mount Ousley Rd and Northfields Ave
  • Mount Ousley Rd
  • Irvine St
  • Northfields Ave (including northbound M1 Princes Mwy on and off ramps)
  • Dumfries Ave

During the work, traffic control and reduced speed limits will be in place at times.

More information is available on the project webpage.

arrangementElements.html:
All heavy vehicles up to and including PBS 1 can use the southern heavy vehicle route via Tompsitt Dr, Edwin Land Pkwy and Ellerton Dr.

All OSOM vehicles over PBS 2 to use the existing approved OSOM route via Pialligo Rd and Yass Rd.

Please note:

Unless specified otherwise, operators of Class 1,2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a Notice must consult their relevant enforceable network map to confirm a suitable approved alternate route. OSOM vehicles operating under the National Class 1 Load Carrying Vehicles Mass and/or Dimension Exemption Notices can use the available detours if they are suitable for their combination. Operators are reminded that under Schedule 8 of the Heavy Vehicle (Mass, Dimension and Loading) National Regulation (NSW), there is a requirement for the driver and operator to scope the proposed route prior to travel to ensure it is suitable for their vehicle.

Operators of Class 1, 2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a Permit must not travel off the approved route listed in the permit unless an updated permit is obtained from the NHVR.

webLinks.linkText: Great Western Highway Road Closure at Victoria Pass

properties.otherAdvice:
Transport for NSW is proactively working to increase the durability and resilience Chifley Rd.

Work will include heavy patching, re-laying asphalt, and refreshing line markings. This work will take place in sections, moving along the road each shift.

Alternating (stop/slow) traffic conditions will be in place to allow motorists to pass in one direction at a time.

These works are likely to cause delays of up to 5 minutes.

For motorists using the Great Western Highway detour via Darling Causeway and Chifley Road, we advise you to allow an additional 35 minutes for your trip - these works and similar works on Darling Causeway are likely to cause total delays of up to 10 minutes. Please exercise extra caution, and follow the directions of signage and traffic controllers.

There will be no work on Saturday nights or public holidays.

webLinks.linkText: Great Western Highway Road Closure at Victoria Pass

properties.otherAdvice:
Transport for NSW is proactively working to increase the durability and resilience of the Darling Causeway.

General speed limit on Darling Causeway is reduced to 70 km/h as of Friday 27 March 2026.

Work will include heavy patching, re-laying asphalt, and refreshing line markings. This work will take place in sections, moving along the causeway each shift.

Alternating (stop/slow) traffic conditions will be in place to allow motorists to pass in one direction at a time.

These works are likely to cause delays of up to 5 minutes.

For motorists using the Great Western Highway detour via Darling Causeway and Chifley Road, we advise you to allow an additional 35 minutes for your trip - these works and similar works on Chifley Rd (beginning 7 April) are likely to cause total delays of up to 10 minutes. Please exercise extra caution, and follow the directions of signage and traffic controllers.

There will be no work on Saturday nights or public holidays.

Yambie Lagoon Rest Area closures for installation of amenities.

7 April - 18 April: eastbound rest area closed

20 April - 2 May: westbound rest area closed

EUROBODALLA REGIONAL HOSPITAL CONSTRUCTION- INTERSECTION UPGRADES

Construction work is underway on the new hospital, including road and intersection upgrades.

Intermittent impacts to traffic will be in place at various times along the Princes Hwy which may include lane closures and stop/slow traffic control.

Alternating (stop/slow) traffic conditions will be in place. Traffic controllers will be on site to direct motorists.

Motorists are advised to allow approximately 10 minutes of additional travel time.

arrangementElements.html: On Wednesday 29 April, the northern side of the Tongarra Road and Terry Street intersection will be closed between 8pm and 4am. Heavy vehicles over 5 tonnes and over 14.5 metres long travelling to or from the north will need to enter/exit the M1 motorway at Yallah via the Princes Highway at Albion Park Rail on this night.

M4 Mwy will be closed westbound west of the Homebush Bay Dr off-ramp for motorists entering from Parramatta Rd/Concord Rd at North Stathfield.

properties.diversions:
Homebush Bay Drive off-ramp, Centenary Dr (southbound) to Arthur St, then Richmond Rd, Marlborough Rd and Centenary Drive (northbound) to Homebush Bay Dr and M4 Mwy on-ramp.

Motorists can also use Marlborough Rd southbound then loop back northbound.

Alternatively, use Parramatta Rd between North Strathfield and Auburn then Silverwater Rd to access the M4 Mwy.
